other_observers: Tony Leukering, Glenn Walbek, Rich Levad, Kim Potter, other RMBO bird monitoring staff;
description: A small, dark, long-winged swift. The bird, seen overhead, appeared dark gray or almost black underneath the body and wings, perhaps a shade lighter on the throat. Wings much longer than body, swept back. Tiny bill nearly invisible, blunt head, and squared-off tail makes body reminiscent of a cigar. Quick, wiry wingbeat typical of Chaetura swifts.Behaviors: The bird appeared to be migrating, flying purposefully towards the north.Call: not heardPlumage: adult
